steps.title: Read two points from the boundary line
steps.content: The boundary line (solid black) separates the shaded region from the unshaded region. Reading directly from the graph, the line passes through (0, −13) on the y-axis and (−10, −11) on the left side.

steps.title: Use the points to solve for t and r
steps.content: The boundary has equation $rx+ty=-65$. Substitute the $y$-intercept $(0,-13)$:$$0r-13t=-65\Rightarrow t=5$$Then substitute $(-10,-11)$:$$-10r-11(5)=-65$$ $$-10r-55=-65\Rightarrow r=1$$

steps.title: Identify r and t, then compute r + t
steps.content: The constants are $r=1$ and $t=5$, so $r+t=1+5=6$. A is correct.
